Portfolio-Level Early Warning System
← Back to dashboard

Portfolio-Level Early Warning System

CoT portfolio-level early warning: extremes, crowding, squeeze/exhaustion, composite stress index.

As of 2026-02-17

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given the any_extreme_share_z of 0.73 and an Early_Warning_Index of 0.003, this setup aligns with range-biased price paths and normal volatility, where the dominant risk is mean reversion, not trend continuation (1).
  • The signal is currently in a Normal regime, indicating typical market functioning with balanced breadth across 361 active markets (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No.

Methodology Applied:

  • Regime classification uses fixed thresholds: Normal (-0.5 to 0.5), Elevated (0.5 to 1.0), and High Alert (>1.0) (1).
  • Extreme positioning is defined by Speculator or Hedger z-scores exceeding ±2.0 standard deviations (1).
  • The composite Early_Warning_Index averages robust z-scores of breadth shares for crowding, squeezes, and exhaustion (1).
  • Latest dataset observation: February 17, 2026 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the stabilization of the Early Warning Index at 0.00, following a decline from "Elevated" levels in late 2025 (1).
  • Internal tension exists between elevated long crowding (0.82 z-score) and subdued short-side interest (-0.91 z-score) (1).
  • Momentum has stabilized, with extreme positioning shares (0.158) remaining well below historical stress levels (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: An index breach above the 0.50 "Elevated" threshold would signal rising systemic reversal risk.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Continued range-bound market functioning supported by neutral aggregate breadth.
  • Upside risk: A volatility compression phase if any_extreme_share_z trends toward the "Subdued" threshold (-0.5).
  • Downside risk: A long liquidation cluster triggered by the current elevated crowded_long_share (0.82) (1).

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks), as positioning breadth captures short-term sentiment imbalances and reversal risks (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium, providing a cross-asset confirmation of stability that offsets specific sector volatility.

Macro Relevance:

  • Macro dimension: Positioning breadth and sentiment-driven liquidity risk.
  • Cycle position: Mid-cycle; positioning is neither extremely lean nor dangerously overextended.
  • Typical interaction: High Alert readings often precede Value-at-Risk (VAR) spikes and deleveraging cycles (1).

Data & References:

  • Latest Data: CFTC Commitments of Traders (CoT) signals for February 17, 2026 (1).
  • Influential Datapoints: any_extreme_share_z (0.73) and crowded_long_share_z (0.82).
  • Additional reliability would be improved by integrating the VIX Market Volatility (2) or the Financial Stress Index (3).

Early Warning System Chart

Portfolio-level stress indicators: extremes, crowding, squeeze/exhaustion, composite index.
